UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user account within the system, used for authentication and authorization (via an external system like Firebase Auth). This entity stores user profile information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

displayName.description: The user's display name or full name.

role.description: The role assigned to the user (e.g., 'Admin', 'Staff'), determining their permissions within the application.

companyProfileId.description: Reference to the CompanyProfile this user is associated with. (Relationship: CompanyProfile 1:N UserProfile)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

CompanyProfile.title: CompanyProfile
CompanyProfile.description: Stores general information and branding details for a company, used across various documents and system settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CompanyProfile entity.

companyName.description: The official registered name of the company.

address.description: The full physical address of the company.

taxId.description: The company's tax identification number (e.g., GST registration number in Maldives).

contactEmail.description: The primary contact email address for the company.

contactPhone.description: The primary contact phone number for the company.

logoUrl.description: The URL to the company's logo image, used for branding on documents.

defaultTaxRate.description: The default tax rate (e.g., GST percentage) applied to new documents created by the company.

currency.description: The default currency used by the company for financial documents (e.g., 'MVR').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the company profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the company profile was last updated.

ownerUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initially created or is the primary owner of this company profile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 CompanyProfile)

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a client or customer of the company, storing their contact and billing details for use in documents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

name.description: The full name or company name of the client.

email.description: The primary email address of the client.

phone.description: The primary phone number of the client.

address.description: The billing or primary physical address of the client.

taxId.description: The client's tax identification number, if applicable for invoicing purposes.

companyProfileId.description: Reference to the CompanyProfile that this client is associated with. (Relationship: CompanyProfile 1:N Client)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was last updated.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a financial document, encompassing both Invoices and Quotations, with common properties.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

documentNumber.description: A unique, auto-generated sequential number for the document (e.g., INV-001 for invoice, QTN-001 for quotation).

type.description: The type of document, either 'Invoice' or 'Quotation'.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client associated with this document. (Relationship: Client 1:N Document)

companyProfileId.description: Reference to the CompanyProfile that issued this document. (Relationship: CompanyProfile 1:N Document)

date.description: The date when the document was issued.

dueDate.description: The due date for payment, primarily applicable for invoices. Null for quotations unless converted.

taxRate.description: The specific tax rate (e.g., 6% GST) applied to this document at the time of creation.

currency.description: The currency used for the document's amounts (e.g., 'MVR').

subtotal.description: The sum of all document item prices multiplied by quantities, before tax calculation.

taxAmount.description: The calculated amount of tax applied to the document's subtotal.

total.description: The final total amount of the document, including subtotal and tax.

status.description: The current status of the document (e.g., 'Draft', 'Sent', 'Paid', 'Overdue' for invoices; 'Draft', 'Sent', 'Accepted', 'Rejected' for quotations).

notes.description: Any additional notes, terms, or conditions for the document.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the document was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the document was last updated.

createdByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this document. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Document)

DocumentItem.title: DocumentItem
DocumentItem.description: Represents a single line item within an Invoice or Quotation document, detailing a product or service.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DocumentItem entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document (Invoice or Quotation) this item belongs to. (Relationship: Document 1:N DocumentItem)

description.description: A textual description of the item or service.

quantity.description: The quantity of the item or service.

price.description: The unit price of the item or service in the document's currency.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the document item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the document item was last updated.

EmailLog.title: EmailLog
EmailLog.description: Records details of emails sent from the application, such as invoices or quotations to clients, for auditing and status tracking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EmailLog entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document that was attached or pertained to this email. (Relationship: Document 1:N EmailLog)

recipientEmail.description: The email address to which the email was sent.

subject.description: The subject line of the sent email.

body.description: The full content body of the sent email, including any AI-generated text.

sentAt.description: Timestamp when the email was sent.

sentByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated sending this email. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N EmailLog)

status.description: The delivery status of the email (e.g., 'Sent', 'Failed', 'Pending').

definition.description: Stores user profile information, accessible primarily by the user themselves. The `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. Includes `companyProfileId` and `role` which are crucial for establishing the user's authorization context in security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores general and branding information for each company. Access is controlled by the `ownerUserId` and other users associated with this `companyId` via their UserProfile. This collection's ID serves as the primary `companyProfileId` for all related subcollections.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique ID of the company profile.

definition.description: Clients associated with a specific company. Access is restricted to users whose `companyProfileId` (from their UserProfile) matches the `companyId` in the path. Includes denormalized `companyProfileId` for authorization independence.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent company.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique ID of the client.

definition.description: Stores financial documents (Invoices and Quotations) issued by a company. Access is restricted to users whose `companyProfileId` matches the `companyId` in the path. Includes denormalized `companyProfileId` and `createdByUserId` for authorization independence and granular rule definition.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent company.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique ID of the document (invoice or quotation).

definition.description: Line items for a specific financial document. Access is inherited from the parent document and company. Includes denormalized `companyProfileId` from the parent document (grandparent company) for authorization independence, eliminating `get()` calls to parent `Document` for access control.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique ID of the grandparent company.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent document.

params.name: documentItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the document line item.

definition.description: Logs of emails sent in relation to a specific financial document. Access is inherited from the parent document and company. Includes denormalized `companyProfileId` from the parent document (grandparent company) and `sentByUserId` for authorization independence.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique ID of the grandparent company.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent document.

params.name: emailLogId
params.description: The unique ID of the email log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for ForgeDocs Management is designed around strong authorization independence, clear data ownership, and efficient query capabilities, prioritizing security and debuggability. The core principle applied is **Authorization Independence via Denormalization**.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
To avoid complex and fragile security rules that rely on `get()` operations to parent documents, key authorization context is denormalized. Specifically:

1.  **User Context:** User profiles are stored in `/user_profiles/{userId}`. Each `UserProfile` contains `companyProfileId` and `role`. While accessing this document for `request.auth.uid` is a `get()` operation within security rules, it's a foundational, one-time context load for the current user, not a hierarchical dependency on the target resource's parent. This allows rules to establish the `requestingUserCompanyId` and `requestingUserRole` to evaluate permissions against any document.
2.  **Company Context:** For all data entities that logically belong to a `CompanyProfile` (e.g., `Client`, `Document`, `DocumentItem`, `EmailLog`), the `companyProfileId` of their associated company is explicitly stored as a field within their own document. This means a `Client` document at `/companies/{companyId}/clients/{clientId}` will have a `companyProfileId` field equal to `{companyId}`. Similarly, a `DocumentItem` at `/companies/{companyId}/documents/{documentId}/items/{documentItemId}` will contain its grandparent's `companyProfileId` field, denormalized from its parent `Document`. This eliminates the need for rules to perform `get()` calls to parent or grandparent documents (e.g., to `/companies/{companyId}/documents/{documentId}`) to determine authorization, making rules atomic, faster, and easier to debug.

**QAPs (Queries as Authorization Primitives):**
This denormalization strategy directly supports QAPs. Since the `companyProfileId` (and for some, `createdByUserId`) is present in every relevant document, `list` operations can be secured effectively. For example, to list all clients for a user's company, a client-side query like `db.collection('companies').doc(userCompanyId).collection('clients').where('companyProfileId', '==', userCompanyId)` combined with a server-side rule like `allow list: if request.auth.uid != null && get(/user_profiles/$(request.auth.uid)).data.companyProfileId == resource.data.companyProfileId;` works robustly. The `resource.data.companyProfileId` is directly available in the document being evaluated, meaning the Firestore security engine can efficiently check permissions without fetching additional documents.

**Structural Segregation and Access Modeling:**
*   **User Profiles (`/user_profiles/{userId}`):** Private data, accessible primarily by the user themselves, with specific admin overrides. `userId` matches `request.auth.uid` for simple ownership rules.
*   **Company Data (`/companies/{companyId}`, `/companies/{companyId}/clients/{clientId}`, `/companies/{companyId}/documents/{documentId}`):** All data under a specific `companyId` shares a homogeneous security posture. Access is granted to users whose `user_profiles/{uid}.companyProfileId` matches the `{companyId}` wildcard in the path, with further distinctions based on their `role` (Admin, Staff) to control `create`/`edit`/`delete` permissions.
*   **Nested Document Data (`/companies/{companyId}/documents/{documentId}/items/{documentItemId}`, `/companies/{companyId}/documents/{documentId}/email_logs/{emailLogId}`):** These subcollections inherit their security context from their `companyId` grandparent and their parent `documentId`, reinforced by the denormalized `companyProfileId` within each item and email log entry.

This design ensures simple, robust, and easily debuggable security rules while supporting scalability and performance requirements for the ForgeDocs Management system.
